headline: SpaceX surges past Amazon, briefly tops Microsoft in market cap
summary: The move builds on an already historic run. SpaceX priced its IPO at $135 per share on June 12, 2026, in what was the largest public offering in history, raising $75 billion initially. Underwriters exercised their greenshoe option on June 15, lifting total proceeds to $85.7 billion. Tuesday's close puts SPCX up roughly 50% from its IPO price in just four trading sessions, a pace that has rattled even the most seasoned market observers.

raw.headline: SpaceX surges past Amazon, briefly tops Microsoft in market cap
raw.summary: The move builds on an already historic run. SpaceX priced its IPO at $135 per share on June 12, 2026, in what was the largest public offering in history, raising $75 billion initially. Underwriters exercised their greenshoe option on June 15, lifting total proceeds to $85.7 billion. Tuesday's close puts SPCX up roughly 50% from its IPO price in just four trading sessions, a pace that has rattled even the most seasoned market observers.

headline: Surging SpaceX overtakes Amazon to become 5th biggest company
summary: SpaceX shares surged again Tuesday, lifting Elon Musk's rocket company above Amazon into fifth place in market value as a record-breaking IPO gives way to a torrid buying frenzy.Shares of SpaceX have soared about 40 percent in three sessions since last week's IPO, which raised a record-breaking $85.7 billion.

headline: Oracle says details in report of failed Microsoft cloud deal 'inaccurate'
summary: Oracle said on Tuesday that details in a Business Insider report on the ‌collapse of its discussions with Microsoft over a ‌potential leasing deal were inaccurate.  The report had said that Microsoft's discussions ​with Oracle regarding a cloud infrastructure leasing deal have fallen apart due to security and compliance concerns.  Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the report, ‌which cited people ⁠familiar with the matter.

headline: SpaceX vaults past Microsoft and Amazon in market value
summary: STORY: Elon Musk's SpaceX is now worth more than Amazon.:: SpaceX:: AmazonShares of the rocket maker soared as much as 17% on Tuesday, catapulting the company's market value past Amazon's and briefly above Microsoft's.That makes SpaceX the fifth most valuable company in the world... just days after its blockbuster public debut.If the gains hold, shares will have jumped more than 60% above the $135 IPO price, giving SpaceX a market cap of around $2.8 trillion. Not bad for a company that reported a net loss last year of nearly $5 billion.The stock rally is likely to continue as SpaceX gains fast-track inclusion in the Nasdaq 100, making it a major holding for passive funds and ETFs that track the index.FTSE Russell and MSCI are also set to add the stock to their indexes, effective later this month.SpaceX options also began trading on Tuesday. More than half a million contracts changed hands in just the first hour, making SpaceX the most heavily traded name ever among companies on their first day of trading options, according to Trade Alert data.The company also made news by announcing on Tuesday that it will buy Anysphere, the startup behind the popular AI coding agent Cursor, in an all-stock deal valued at $60 billion.The acquisition aims to give SpaceX's xAI a stronger hold in the revenue-generating business of AI coding.

raw.headline: SpaceX vaults past Microsoft and Amazon in market value
raw.summary: STORY: Elon Musk's SpaceX is now worth more than Amazon.:: SpaceX:: AmazonShares of the rocket maker soared as much as 17% on Tuesday, catapulting the company's market value past Amazon's and briefly above Microsoft's.That makes SpaceX the fifth most valuable company in the world... just days after its blockbuster public debut.If the gains hold, shares will have jumped more than 60% above the $135 IPO price, giving SpaceX a market cap of around $2.8 trillion. Not bad for a company that reported a net loss last year of nearly $5 billion.The stock rally is likely to continue as SpaceX gains fast-track inclusion in the Nasdaq 100, making it a major holding for passive funds and ETFs that track the index.FTSE Russell and MSCI are also set to add the stock to their indexes, effective later this month.SpaceX options also began trading on Tuesday. More than half a million contracts changed hands in just the first hour, making SpaceX the most heavily traded name ever among companies on their first day of trading options, according to Trade Alert data.The company also made news by announcing on Tuesday that it will buy Anysphere, the startup behind the popular AI coding agent Cursor, in an all-stock deal valued at $60 billion.The acquisition aims to give SpaceX's xAI a stronger hold in the revenue-generating business of AI coding.

headline: The Dow Is Above 52,000. It's Only Been 12 Days Since It Hit 51,000.

headline: The Mag 7 plus these three AI companies make up the new Fab 10
summary: The Magnificent Seven plus SpaceX (SPCX), Anthropic (ANTH.PVT), and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) equal the Fab 10. Could this trio of AI mega-IPOs bring enthusiasm back to the tech space previously dominated by the Mag 7? Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré and Innovator From Goldman Sachs Asset Management chief investment strategist Tim Urbanowicz weigh in on the renewed investor sentiment these companies could bring to the AI trade. The Magnificent Seven consists of Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL, GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA).

headline: Former Microsoft chief Barclay joins British fintech unicorn Zilch
summary: One of Britain's leading fintech unicorns will this week name Microsoft's former UK boss to its board as it continues to seek acquisition targets ahead of an eventual public listing.  Sky News understands that Zilch, which has been valued at about $2bn, will this week announce that Dame Clare Barclay has been appointed as a non-executive director.  Dame Clare, who recently left Microsoft after 27 years which included stints as UK country manager and president of enterprise and industry in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, also serves as chair of the government's Industrial Strategy Advisory Council.

headline: Tech Weekly: A blockbuster IPO, a sweeping online block
summary: STORY: From a record-breaking IPO to a far-reaching online ban, this is Tech Weekly. :: Tech WeeklySpaceX on Thursday priced the biggest-ever U.S. initial public offering, raising a record $75 billion. It’s made Elon Musk’s rocket and spacecraft manufacturer one of the world’s most valuable companies.Selling 555.56 million shares at $135 apiece, the company was valued at $1.77 trillion. Thursday’s pricing supports Musk's most ambitious project yet - even as some analysts question whether its lofty valuation is justified.The company said Monday its underwriters had exercised the so-called "greenshoe" option to purchase additional shares.Microsoft has been sued by shareholders who say they've been defrauded. They’ve said the tech giant inflated its stock price by failing to disclose slowing growth in its Azure cloud business.And failing to disclose the need to spend billions of dollars on AI infrastructure. The proposed class action, filed Friday, was prompted by Microsoft shares falling 10% on January 29 after an earnings report.About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.Microsoft said on Monday it believes the claims are "without merit”. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would ban social media sites for under-16s..And impose restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms for the age group. This amounts to some of the world's most far-reaching online restrictions, with the ban coming into place by next spring.He said this would help protect children online while pushing back against the power of big technology companies."At the moment, strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger, an adult that you don't know anything about?”Ford and other automakers are scrambling to obtain U.S. government authorization to continue selling certain models - despite being on the market for years. It’s due to a U.S. ban on Chinese-developed and -maintained software in connected vehicles. Ford told Reuters it’s asked the U.S. Commerce Department for authorization to continue importing its China-built Lincoln Nautilus SUV.And said despite its tech being built in the U.S., its installation in China now requires government approval. Lawmakers have ​proposed making the rules even tougher.

raw.headline: Tech Weekly: A blockbuster IPO, a sweeping online block
raw.summary: STORY: From a record-breaking IPO to a far-reaching online ban, this is Tech Weekly. :: Tech WeeklySpaceX on Thursday priced the biggest-ever U.S. initial public offering, raising a record $75 billion. It’s made Elon Musk’s rocket and spacecraft manufacturer one of the world’s most valuable companies.Selling 555.56 million shares at $135 apiece, the company was valued at $1.77 trillion. Thursday’s pricing supports Musk's most ambitious project yet - even as some analysts question whether its lofty valuation is justified.The company said Monday its underwriters had exercised the so-called "greenshoe" option to purchase additional shares.Microsoft has been sued by shareholders who say they've been defrauded. They’ve said the tech giant inflated its stock price by failing to disclose slowing growth in its Azure cloud business.And failing to disclose the need to spend billions of dollars on AI infrastructure. The proposed class action, filed Friday, was prompted by Microsoft shares falling 10% on January 29 after an earnings report.About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.Microsoft said on Monday it believes the claims are "without merit”. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would ban social media sites for under-16s..And impose restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms for the age group. This amounts to some of the world's most far-reaching online restrictions, with the ban coming into place by next spring.He said this would help protect children online while pushing back against the power of big technology companies."At the moment, strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger, an adult that you don't know anything about?”Ford and other automakers are scrambling to obtain U.S. government authorization to continue selling certain models - despite being on the market for years. It’s due to a U.S. ban on Chinese-developed and -maintained software in connected vehicles. Ford told Reuters it’s asked the U.S. Commerce Department for authorization to continue importing its China-built Lincoln Nautilus SUV.And said despite its tech being built in the U.S., its installation in China now requires government approval. Lawmakers have ​proposed making the rules even tougher.

headline: EeroQ Makes World-First Breakthrough in Electron Qubits Floating on Helium
summary: EeroQ, the quantum computing company, today announced the first-ever demonstration of the strong coupling of a microwave photon to the charge qubit state of an electron on helium, a landmark result that establishes the electron-on-helium (eHe) approach to quantum computing as a reality and introduces a powerful new contender in the global race to build a practical quantum computer. The results have been peer reviewed and published in Nature Physics.

The results have been peer reviewed and published in Nature Physics.
